sourceMovie.title: Love
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Love works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Dreamers
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Love clicked for you, The Dreamers (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Love?
faq.answer: Try The Dreamers, Desire, Jules and Jim—each shares DNA with Love while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Love?
faq.answer: Love (2015) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Love worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
